prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of ferrocene (21 atoms: 10 C, 10 H, 1 Fe) computed at the GFN2-xTB level (semi-empirical tight-binding, xtb backend) in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry taken from the user-supplied .xyz file (no re-optimization). N±1 states: cation (charge +1, mult 2) and anion (charge −1, mult 2). Partial charges via Mulliken (GFN2-xTB).

Headline result: Fe (atom 11) is both the most electrophilic site (f⁺ = 0.22885456) and the most nucleophilic site (f⁻ = 0.25938191), with a negative dual descriptor (−0.030527), indicating net nucleophilic character at the iron centre. All C and H atoms carry small, nearly equal f⁺/f⁻ values with positive dual descriptors (~+0.0015), consistent with their role as electron-donating Cp-ring atoms.

Integrity: status ok, trustworthy true. Charge conservation checks passed (Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000). No warnings reported by the engine.
